sentences.text: Hey, Phillip, can you hear me okay?
Can you hear me?
I think you're just fine, how you doing today?
How's the week going?
So far, so good.
Yeah, so far so good as well.
It's always a little funny when you've taken some time, and it's a long weekend, and… getting back into the swing of things, especially now.
Everybody, I feel like, is back in the seat, and they're really focused on finishing the year off, so…
Yeah, really throws you off those couple days.
Right?
Did you have a chance to get out of town over the long weekend, or did you just take it easy and stay local?
Just kind of hung out, nothing crazy.
Yeah.
Yeah, that's nice.
Just gotta relax, take care of some chores, that's what we did over here.
Pretty much, yeah.
Well, thank you so much for being so flexible with your time.
I really apologize for missing you the other week, but psyched to just touch base on, kind of, what we're up to this year.
see where we are, feedback-wise from your team, make sure you guys have everything you need, and then just kind of show you what we're working on the rest of the year.
We've got some fun things coming into the tool, I'm sure you've seen how we've iterated.
So, love to get your… continue to get your feedback so we can inform that roadmap, but that's really what I'm looking for today, just to kind of let you know what we're up to.
And make sure we're all set through the re-year.
Anything you'd like to add to this kind of general agenda, general walkthrough here?
No, I think I'm good.
Cool, alright.
So we'll jump right in.
Just wanted to quickly kind of highlight what's going on, what I've seen in the usage.
Feels like we've got a lot of engaged folks.
You're up there with our top users, Ariana, Amy, Hunter, names that come up and log in quite frequently.
Yep, those are all… they're all new, and they're using it every day, basically, so…
Excellent.
I did see that they were newer users, but I'm excited to see that they're engaged and logged in.
Is there anyone else that we want to assign research seats to?
Because you guys do have 20 seats.
Not yet.
We are in the process of kind of revamping our dev team.
Yeah.
And… that should happen… the next 10 days or so, have more clarity there, and then I will…
Timely.
I can get you a list.
Yeah, they're all here to.
Definitely.
kind of… Doing other things, but they're trying to finalize that.
Plan.
What's the big initiative there?
Why the shake-up, or are you.
It's just, yeah, we have a lot more people now, so we're just kind of split it up into regions, as opposed to just having.
Sure.
people, so… so we have… West, South, Mid-Atlantic, and Florida.
Okay, excellent.
ones.
Are we expecting a lot of growth in those regions, or across the board?
Yeah, I mean, we have… 57 properties under contract, plus another 28 under construction.
And then 32 opened, so yeah.
Busy time for you guys.
And that's all to close before the end of the year?
Most likely not.
Okay.
Probably within the next 12 months, those will close.
Sure.
No.
Good for you guys.
It's always good to be busy, always good to be growing, especially if you can manage it in this economy, right?
Oh yeah, exactly.
Are all those properties gonna come into ApartmentIQ, or do you guys have a… kind of a thesis for what apartment… what properties do you come into IQ?
They will… they will all be in there.
Excellent.
as we go, I think, I mean, I've built out some of the… research… I'm pulling up mine right now.
I've built out the research on a lot of the ones that are under construction right now.
3, 6, 9, 10… and actually, one of the research ones just went live, so I can.
Okay.
Probably get that.
Moved into the live category.
Definitely, yeah, shoot me a note and we'll get that done.
Easy.
I don't know the best way to do it.
I'm just gonna send it in the chat right here, just cause…
Yeah, shoot it over in the chat, and I'll make a note, I'll copy it myself as well on my end.
That one.
That one right there, Panama City.
Wonderful.
Copying that, and I should have that updated for you.
It's Thursday today, by Monday, I'm thinking, based on the current queue.
Do you need…
They have a…
Maybe the actual website.
I was about to… yeah, that's where I was going.
I was gonna ask, do we have the website, live leasing enabled?
There you go.
Sensational.
I will get it done.
Cool.
Alright.
And that one just opened.
Two weeks ago?
A week ago?
Nice.
Are you gonna head down there to, I guess, do the grand opening, or is that not your…
You're.
Nope.
really my… my thing.
We'll get there eventually, but it's, like, 9 hours away, so it's, pretty sorry.
Sure.
Pass on that one.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
So then I'll shoot you a note if I don't hear from you, but it sounds like I will, to make sure everybody has their research seats assigned.
Don't want those to go unused.
Great, alright.
Priorities for the rest of the year, sounds like we're growing.
Wanted to kind of use… I like to use a slide just to hear if there's anything, maybe that I can expect, from a major dispositions.
You know, our unit's going to be changing quite a lot?
Is there any additional support you need from us?
You know, maybe with these folks coming on, additional training we want to run, happy to do that kind of in small group settings, too.
Yeah, I can definitely talk to… so, Taylor, who's one of our development analysts, he sits in our office here.
Once I roll it out to him, I will probably see what he thinks the best is to roll it out to the other research side.
I… he's… he's on vacation this weekend, but I will talk to him next week and see what he wants to do in that.
Sure.
But that would be the biggest thing, and then I mentioned all the… The new one's coming on.
We'll have… I'm going through the list… of the ones that are in my research right now.
1, 2, 3, 4… 5, 6… 6 of them will open by the end of the year?
Maybe 7.
Excellent.
Wow.
So those will be in the next 4 months, so…
Great!
We have plenty of room for them to come right in.
I believe we're sitting somewhere around 8,300 units, 8,400 units or so.
plenty of room to get these units in.
So, that'll be easy.
I always like to ask, is there… how is ApartmentIQ kind of fitting in within your tech stack right now?
Especially as, kind of, we move through budget season?
Are there other tools you guys are working with, or other tools you're considering?
As you kind of evaluate your tech stack?
Not really.
We use this, especially the data we get from you guys on a daily basis.
Yeah, I mean, we use it for everything, so… It's involved.
QA process?
Formal one, no.
Informal, yeah, if we notice something.
But it's really just, like, a couple of us that are… Doing it to that extent, where we would be, like, examining it.
We've noticed a couple times where the specials are different, but we just reach out and you guys fix it within, like, a day or so, not a big deal.
Okay.
No, other than that, I mean, the biggest thing that we would love at some point is to add the fees, like, mandatory fees, which I think we've talked about before.
I know, it's super hard to do.
But that's just the only thing that we do, like, on the side.
We have had that come up a couple times, and I'll walk you through the roadmap and what we have confirmed through the next couple weeks.
It has made it there and then gotten deprioritized for.
Yeah.
other initiatives.
Probably worth… it's worth, it's way more work than it's worth, I would guess.

It is a lot of work, especially because it would require kind of building in a mechanism, and just because I've been privy to some of the conversations, I've copied some of your actual verbiage into some of our proposals, and what kind of happens is our big deal is the public data, right?
Our big deal is the integrity of the data.
If we allow anybody to go ahead and manually manipulate it, now we gotta create a mechanism that That stops that from being shared.
Yeah.
Right?
So there's just that kind of… piece that isn't really fleshed out yet.
Plus, as you've probably seen, we've launched a few other products now.
So… Soon, we're gonna have some more developers come free, as those products, come to market, and their capacity's opened up, so we'll see what Q1 looks like, but for now.
It is tough, but I do… we do have that noted.
like, for now, I have it in Excel, so it works.
We make it work, so…
Gotcha.
Cool.
Alright, and then… that's kind of what I wanted to get out of here.
Glad IQ is working for you, and kind of, it sounds like, is that primary tool.
Yep.
Product roadmap.
So what we've brought in, kind of this summer.
since we talked, I know we went over cancels and denials, I believe, but property class, improved lease-up logic was changed, that was a big one.
Something that we kind of learned, I guess, just from our years of doing this now, is that when lease-ups come in, we should count occupancy from zero up, rather than from 100% down.
Which is what we do for stabilized properties, but… so that's hopefully made a big difference.
you know, anything here that you see, BTRs, for instance, that was a big one that came out.
Anything here speaking to you in particular, or anything you've noticed so far as you use the tool that's been helpful?
We've started with the… we… we have some BTR product that is coming on, so we've used some of that.
Excellent.
I mean, yeah, we have the pre-lease percentage.
We've used that.
The property class, we definitely use.
Yes, there's… we've definitely used a lot of those.
Beautiful.
Anything else besides fees that comes to mind, let us know.
We really are trying to, build these tools out based on what you guys are saying.
Yeah.
New market survey layout in the Excel function, as well as the PDF, just to kind of call out some of these calculated versus observed metrics, just to make sure everybody knows, hey.
That's how we calculate and project some of these.
Sounds like you guys go through the data, you and a few other users, and know when to kind of give it another look.
But that's other things we're doing here to just make sure everybody knows about this and understand.
Where these numbers come from.
Sounds like pre-list has been helpful for you.
The market survey switcher, was kind of a quality of life improvement.
Especially if you're working maybe with the same couple.
What starts to happen is, in the top left-hand corner, your recent surveys will populate here.
I believe 5 will populate here.
So, folks that work within a portfolio, folks that are constantly using the same couple of surveys, it's just a little bit easier to navigate through.
Makes sense.
And then built rewards.

This is something we heard is coming more and more into focus, kind of getting an understanding of which properties, which companies are accepting built rewards, and kind of participating in that program.
So now you'll see that listed as well.
That went live Last week, or… no, this week.
September 1st, so this is brand new.
And then what's coming?

So, senior housing, being able to identify that, much like we do with the BTR functionality, being able to filter for those in the Explore function, in the Edit Comes function, so that it's easier to see these assets when they're relevant.

Custom sort order of comps, another kind of quality of life function that's gonna piggyback onto the other quality of life functions, being able to custom sort your comps based on what's relevant to you, rather than the default currently, which is by a metric.
order.
Hopefully that'll be helpful.
In scope.
portfolio reporting, so you guys, you mentioned to me that we're kind of moving folks into regions.
Perhaps this starts to be helpful, and maybe we can tack that on to the current groups functionality when you manage users.
We can create groups of specific properties, so we could say, this is the western portfolio, the eastern portfolio, and so on, and then assign users to those.
I was actually about to do that today.
Let me know if you need a hand with that, actually.
If you have maybe an Excel sheet, and you know what properties belong in what group, I'm happy to have our support team take care of that for you.
Cool.
I will check it out.
It seems pretty simple, so…
Yeah, I hope it is.
We hope it is.
We really pride ourselves on everything being pretty accessible, but this is kind of that next iteration.
So, we've been… trying to build out portfolio reporting a while now.
The first step to getting that done was having that group functionality.
Now we'll be able to say, alright, everybody in the Western group, not only are you in this group automatically, but you'll get a full report of what's going on here at a glance.
So really expanding on the portfolio functionality and exporting there.
And then finally, the big one that we're really excited about… whoops, I skipped one.
Forecasting.
So you may have seen, our CEO, Alec, posted something on LinkedIn about this just last week.
But we're starting to get into… we launched Daylight, which is our revenue management software.
We have premium functionality coming to the existing Explore tool, that's going to allow us to see market dynamics, to see a lot more demographic information, and ultimately rank growth forecasting.
So, we're starting a closed beta here with two of our partners right now.
But very soon, we'll have this coming out for everybody in the Explore functionality.
We were just talking about something similar to this today.
That's funny.
Yeah?
Alright.
Just, like, seeing where we…
mine.
Like, one of our… one of our cities, we were just checking to see, like, where… how much we should be… Expecting it to grow in the next year or two, so…
When you're forecasting, do you do that at the… at the MSA level, or is it in much more, Is it down to the city level or a specific zip code?
Yeah.
depends.
All of our properties are kind of, like, in… far suburban areas, so, like.
messy.
level.
Doesn't necessarily reflect
So, like, the one we were just looking at is in Plant City, Florida, which is, like.
not even suburban Tampa, it's, like, the next level out from Tampa.
So it, like, gets, like, the very edge of the Tampa crowd.
So, like, if we looked at Tampa numbers, it's one thing.
Doesn't really reflect what it would do there, so… we have to be a little more creative.
Sure.
What other… I mean, are you using… is it CoStar now that you're using to do that, or…
No, we're literally just looking at other properties using.
Oh, snap.
data, just saying, like, okay, there's 3 other properties in the area.
the next town out is X dollars cheaper, the next town closer to Tampa is more expensive, and we're just kind of like… Trying to maneuver our way into it.
Okay.

Let's keep this in mind, then, maybe next quarter as we flesh out, because our first iteration here is just at the MSA level, and we totally understand That, in your case, you know, the MSA level is not going to be as helpful.
But maybe we can get some feedback from you, some direction to get this a little bit hyper-focused on some of those towns that start to be helpful for you.
Yeah, cool.
And that's, that's kind of the big thing.
Next steps, I guess it sounds like.
Let me send me that list of folks when your dev team gets sorted, so I can get their access.
Happy to talk about scheduling a couple of training sessions for them as well.
We can keep them really, small group if you want to do… it sounded like there was 10 or so, if you want to do 5 and 5 or something like that, happy to do that.
It'll eventually be 10, I think it's, like, 6 right now, but I think we're hiring for a few more, yeah.
Excellent.
And please, you know, keep the feedback coming, Philip.
Let me know if there's anything else I can help with.
The data sync has been totally cool, no interruptions, everything is working there the way you expect it to.
Yeah, we've had some hiccups on our end, but nothing to do with you guys.
Just technical glitches on our end.
Sure, let us know if there's anything we could be helpful with, or if maybe there's a method of delivery that is causing that.
Let us know, and we can see what we can iron out.
All good right now?
Appreciate it, Phil.
Anything else on your end before we split?
I don't think so.
Cool.
Easy.
Easy does it today.
Enjoy the rest of your week, and have a great weekend, alright?
You too, Phil, we'll talk soon.
Take care.
Bye now.
That's it.

conversation.title: Hillpointe <> Apt IQ - Q3 Check In
